skill.name: artifacts-builder
skill.description: Create multi-component Claude artifacts with React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui. Use it when an artifact needs state, routing, reusable UI components, or a single bundled HTML deliverable.
skill.summary: Build complex Claude artifacts with a React and shadcn/ui workflow.
skill.author: ArtemisAI

security_audit.summary: The high-severity weak-crypto detections are false positives from Apache license text, CSS variable names, and documentation wording. The skill does contain legitimate shell scripts that install npm packages, create and overwrite project files, and remove build output, so publication should include a clear execution warning.

medium_findings.title: Shell Scripts Install External Packages
medium_findings.description: The initialization and bundling scripts run npm and pnpm commands that fetch packages from external registries. This is expected for a frontend scaffolding tool, but it exposes users to network and supply-chain risk when executed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The package-manager commands are explicit and intentional. They are not malicious by themselves, but they require network access and execute third-party package lifecycle behavior.

medium_findings.title: Project Files Are Created and Overwritten
medium_findings.description: The setup script writes configuration and source files, edits project metadata, and extracts a bundled component archive into the generated project. This is normal scaffolding behavior, but users should run it only in intended project directories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes are visible and limited to the generated project workflow. The risk is operational rather than evidence of hidden abuse.

medium_findings.title: Build Cleanup Deletes Local Output Paths
medium_findings.description: The bundling script removes dist and bundle.html before rebuilding. The paths are fixed and local, but accidental execution in the wrong project can delete existing build output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The rm command targets fixed relative paths, which reduces abuse potential. It is still a real destructive filesystem operation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the Apache License and shadcn/ui documentation. No evidence found of credential exfiltration, tracking, or unauthorized network calls from these references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or schema references. They do not receive local data in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto detections occur in license language, frontmatter text, or CSS theme names such as destructive. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, hashing, encryption, or signature verification.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the referenced lines shows prose and CSS configuration, not security-sensitive cryptographic code. The finding category does not match the semantic context.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are Benign Guidance
low_findings.description: The system-reconnaissance matches occur in style guidance and optional testing guidance. No evidence found of commands that enumerate hosts, users, processes, credentials, or network configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced text is documentation for artifact design and optional visualization. It does not request environment discovery or collection of sensitive system details.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Detection Is Documentation Syntax
low_findings.description: The dynamic import finding points to a JSDoc type annotation inside generated Tailwind configuration. No evidence found of runtime dynamic module loading from user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is emitted into a config file as a type comment. It is not a dynamic import expression that executes code.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs users to execute shell scripts and those scripts run package managers, build tools, sed, tar, and rm. This is necessary for the advertised workflow but should be reviewed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell execution path is explicit in both documentation and scripts. The commands support the tool purpose, but they materially change the local environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Artifact Bundling
dangerous_patterns.description: The bundling script inlines built assets into bundle.html after a Parcel build. Users should review generated output before sharing it in conversations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The bundling behavior is clear and expected. The risk depends on project dependencies and artifact code content rather than hidden logic in the script.

content.user_title: Build Rich Claude Artifacts with React
content.value_statement: Complex artifacts often need more structure than a single HTML file can provide. This skill creates a React, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui project and bundles it into a shareable artifact.
actual_capabilities: Initializes a React 18 and TypeScript project with Vite.
Adds Tailwind CSS 3.4.1 and shadcn/ui theme configuration.
Installs Radix UI and related component dependencies.
Extracts prepackaged shadcn/ui components into the source tree.
Configures path aliases for TypeScript and Vite.
Bundles the finished app into one self-contained HTML file.
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or newer and a working package manager environment.
Downloads npm packages during setup and bundling.
Best suited for complex artifacts, not simple static snippets.
Generated artifacts still need human review before sharing.

use_cases.title: Prototype Interactive Product Demos
use_cases.description: Create a polished React artifact with stateful controls, dialogs, tables, and responsive layout.

use_cases.title: Build Shareable Frontend Tools
use_cases.description: Package a small calculator, dashboard, editor, or workflow tool into one HTML artifact.

use_cases.title: Standardize Artifact Scaffolding
use_cases.description: Use a repeatable setup for teams that build Claude artifacts with the same UI stack.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Artifact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use artifacts-builder to create a React artifact named project-planner. Add a simple task board with shadcn/ui buttons and cards.

prompt_templates.title: Add Stateful Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an artifact with filters, tabs, and editable rows. Use React state and shadcn/ui components where they fit.

prompt_templates.title: Bundle a Finished Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Bundle the current React artifact into one HTML file. Summarize the output location and any build warnings.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-View Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-view analytics artifact with navigation, charts, forms, and saved local state. Keep the final output shareable as one HTML artifact.

output_examples.input: Create a customer onboarding dashboard artifact.
output_examples.output: A React artifact project with dashboard sections, forms, shadcn/ui controls, Tailwind styling, and a bundled HTML deliverable.

output_examples.input: Bundle my completed artifact for sharing.
output_examples.output: A single bundle.html file that contains the built application assets and can be shared as a Claude artifact.

output_examples.input: Start a reusable UI-heavy artifact from scratch.
output_examples.output: A configured Vite project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, path aliases, and preinstalled shadcn/ui components.

best_practices: Run the scripts inside a dedicated project directory.
Review package changes and generated files before committing them.
Use this workflow when component structure or state management is needed.
anti_patterns: Do not run the bundling script in an unrelated repository root.
Do not use this skill for a simple one-file static mockup.
Do not share generated HTML before checking content and behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a React and TypeScript project configured for Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui artifacts.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. The scripts install npm packages during project setup and bundling.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is suitable for publication with a warning because it runs shell scripts and modifies local files.

faq.question: When should I avoid it?
faq.answer: Avoid it when a simple HTML, CSS, or JSX snippet is enough.

faq.question: What is the final artifact format?
faq.answer: The bundling script produces one HTML file with application assets inlined.
